title: assistant-clarify Skill Behavior Eval Fixtures
description: Provider-neutral offline eval cases for the assistant-clarify skill's clarification-first workflow.
recommended_use: Run each case with the assistant-clarify skill instructions loaded.
Compare the local response against the expected behavior, pass criteria, and failure signals.
Use deterministic substring checks as offline proxies for contract adherence, not semantic grading.

cases.title: Multi-intent prompt asks bounded clarification
cases.purpose: Checks that mixed implementation, documentation, and priority requests are clarified before execution.
cases.prompt: I need you to fix the onboarding flow, maybe write docs too, and make the skill stuff less confusing before the demo. Not sure if the bug or docs matters more.
setup_context: The assistant-clarify skill instructions are active.
No prior task state resolves whether the primary outcome is a code change, documentation, or planning brief.
The user gives urgency and multiple possible deliverables but no target files or priority order.
expected_behavior: Reflects the likely goal without calling the prompt messy or blaming the user.
Identifies that the request contains multiple possible outcomes and still needs clarification.
Produces a structured brief with knowns, unknowns, assumptions, constraints, and likely deliverables.
Asks one to three high-yield clarifying questions with bounded choices or a recommended default.
States a provisional execution target and marks the status as needs clarification.
Does not begin implementation, documentation edits, or broad refactoring before the ambiguity is reduced.
pass_criteria: The response includes a concise interpretation or likely goal.
The structured brief separates known facts from unknowns and assumptions.
The clarifying question offers concrete options instead of a generic request for clarification.
The response preserves momentum by recommending a default or next target.
No file edits or irreversible work are proposed as already started.
fail_signals: Asks only an open-ended clarification with no options.
Starts implementing an arbitrary onboarding or documentation change.
Assumes a broad skill-system refactor is desired.
Labels the user's prompt as chaotic, messy, confusing, or incoherent.

forbidden_substrings: I will do a broad refactor

cases.title: Compressed request produces structured brief
cases.purpose: Checks that a compressed request is converted into an actionable brief with assumptions and a recommended default.
cases.prompt: This might be two separate asks: refactor the memory hook and write a research note, but I don't want a huge process. Help me turn it into something actionable.
setup_context: The assistant-clarify skill instructions are active.
The user asks for help structuring the work rather than immediate implementation.
The request names two possible outcomes and a constraint against unnecessary process.
expected_behavior: States the likely goal as turning the compressed request into an actionable execution target.
Builds a structured brief that includes knowns, unknowns, assumptions, constraints, and likely deliverables.
Separates the refactor and research-note possibilities so the user can choose a primary outcome.
Asks at most three clarifying questions and includes a recommendation or default path.
Identifies the execution target as provisional until the user confirms the priority.
Reports status as needs clarification rather than ready to implement.
pass_criteria: The response contains a structured brief with distinct sections for knowns and unknowns.
The response includes assumptions the assistant would otherwise have to make.
The response includes a bounded choice between refactor-first and research-note-first paths.
The response includes a concrete recommendation that keeps the process lightweight.
The response does not over-expand the scope into a full workflow plan.
fail_signals: Treats the request as already ready for BUILD.
Asks more than three clarification questions.
Provides only a taxonomy or lecture without an execution target.
Ignores the user's constraint against a huge process.
